title: bean: an overview
text: remaining vignettes walk step detail.
code: data(origin_dat_prepared, package = "bean") env_vars <- c("bio_1", "bio_4", "bio_12", "bio_15")  # 1. Pick an objective grid resolution from the data res <- find_env_resolution(origin_dat_prepared, env_vars = env_vars) res #> --- Bean environmental grid resolution --- #> Bandwidth selector: sheather-jones #>  #>  variable resolution #>     bio_1 0.08274934 #>     bio_4 0.85162273 #>    bio_12 2.25907105 #>    bio_15 0.07070831  # 2. Thin in environmental space thinned <- thin_env_nd(   data = origin_dat_prepared,   env_vars = env_vars,   grid_resolution = res$suggested_resolution,   seed = 1 ) thinned #> --- Bean Stochastic Thinning Results --- #>  #> Thinned 1024 original points to 78 points. #> This represents a retention of 7.6% of the data. #>  #> --------------------------------------

title: 2. Environmental thinning
text: find_env_resolution() selects kernel-density bandwidth environmental variable. bandwidth scale empirical density observations becomes smooth, statistically defensible choice edge length environmental grid cell. default selector Sheather–Jones plug-estimator (Sheather & Jones, 1991); Silverman’s rule ("silverman") Scott’s rule ("scott") also available. Visualise per-variable kernel density chosen bandwidth:
code: res <- find_env_resolution(   data     = origin_dat_prepared,   env_vars = env_vars,   method   = "sheather-jones" ) res #> --- Bean environmental grid resolution --- #> Bandwidth selector: sheather-jones #>  #>  variable resolution #>     bio_1 0.08274934 #>     bio_4 0.85162273 #>    bio_12 2.25907105 #>    bio_15 0.07070831 plot(res)

title: 2. Environmental thinning
text: thin_env_nd() randomly retains exactly one occurrence per occupied grid cell. seed makes selection reproducible without disturbing global random state.
code: thinned_stochastic <- thin_env_nd(   data            = origin_dat_prepared,   env_vars        = env_vars,   grid_resolution = res$suggested_resolution,   seed            = 1 ) thinned_stochastic #> --- Bean Stochastic Thinning Results --- #>  #> Thinned 1024 original points to 78 points. #> This represents a retention of 7.6% of the data. #>  #> --------------------------------------

title: 2. Environmental thinning
text: thin_env_center() replaces occupied cell single point geometric centre cell. randomness, result exactly reproducible.
code: thinned_deterministic <- thin_env_center(   data            = origin_dat_prepared,   env_vars        = env_vars,   grid_resolution = res$suggested_resolution ) thinned_deterministic #> --- Bean Deterministic Thinning Results --- #>  #> Thinned 1024 original points to 78 unique grid cell centers. #> This represents a retention of 7.6% of the data. #>  #> --------------------------------------

title: 2. Environmental thinning
text: plot_bean() overlays thinned points original cloud scatterplot matrix.
code: plot_bean(   original_data  = origin_dat_prepared,   thinned_object = thinned_stochastic,   env_vars       = env_vars )

title: 3. Niche modeling
code: origin_ellipse <- fit_ellipsoid(   data     = origin_dat_prepared,   env_vars = env_vars,   method   = "covmat",   level    = 0.95 ) origin_ellipse #> -- Bean Environmental Niche Ellipsoid -- #> Method      : covmat #> Dimensions  : 4 (bio_1, bio_4, bio_12, bio_15) #> Level       : 95.00% #> Points used : 1024  (inside: 947, 92.5%) #> Centroid: #>      bio_1      bio_4     bio_12     bio_15  #>   24.47107  179.68260 1191.05273   76.98212

title: 3. Niche modeling
text: interactive 3-D view, install optional package rgl call plot(origin_ellipse, dims = c(1, 2, 3)). rgl installed, function falls back 2-D plot first two requested variables.
code: plot(origin_ellipse, dims = c("bio_1", "bio_12"))

title: 3. Niche modeling
code: stochastic_ellipse <- fit_ellipsoid(   data     = thinned_stochastic$thinned_data,   env_vars = env_vars ) plot(stochastic_ellipse, dims = c("bio_1", "bio_12")) deterministic_ellipse <- fit_ellipsoid(   data     = thinned_deterministic$thinned_points,   env_vars = env_vars ) plot(deterministic_ellipse, dims = c("bio_1", "bio_12"))

title: 3. Niche modeling
text: predict() returns Mahalanobis distance Gaussian suitability score. works data.frame directly terra::SpatRaster.
code: library(terra) #> terra 1.9.27 env <- terra::rast(system.file("extdata", "thai_env.tif", package = "bean"))  pred <- predict(   object                 = origin_ellipse,   newdata                = env,   include_suitability    = TRUE,   suitability_truncated  = TRUE,   include_mahalanobis    = FALSE ) plot(pred)

what: Data Thinning of Species Occurrences in Environmental Space
title: Data Thinning of Species Occurrences in Environmental Space
text: Paanwaris Paansri Luis E. Escobar

title: Data Thinning of Species Occurrences in Environmental Space
text: bean package provides tool address fundamental challenge species distribution modeling (SDM, ecological niche modeling, ENM): sampling bias. Occurrence records species rarely collected systematic, stratified process. Instead, often cluster easily accessible areas (like roads cities) well-studied research sites. spatial bias can translate environmental bias, model incorrectly learns species associated environmental conditions heavily sampled areas, rather true ecological requirements. bean tackles problem thinning occurrence data environmental space. goal create uniform distribution points across species’ observed environmental niche, reducing influence densely clustered records. allows construction accurate fundamental niche volume, can projected geographic space create less biased prediction area environmental suitability. name bean reflects core principle method: ensuring “pod” (grid cell environmental space) contains specified number “beans” (occurrence points).

title: Data Thinning of Species Occurrences in Environmental Space
text: bean operates shifting focus geographic space environmental space: Environmental Gridding: Divides environmental hypercube “pods”. Objective Thinning: Reduces clusters specified density per pod. Niche Delineation: Fits ellipsoids thinned data define fundamental niche. Projection: Maps corrected niche back geographic space less biased predictions.

title: Data Thinning of Species Occurrences in Environmental Space
text: development version bean can installed GitHub: load package:
code: # Install devtools if needed if (!require("devtools")) install.packages("devtools")  # Install bean devtools::install_github("paanwaris/bean") library(bean)

title: Data Thinning of Species Occurrences in Environmental Space
text: typical bean workflow consists key steps:

title: Data Thinning of Species Occurrences in Environmental Space
text: prepare_bean() function cleans raw occurrence data removing missing coordinates extracting environmental values raster layers. ensures subsequent analyses use clean, scaled dataset. See Preparing bean vignette.

title: Data Thinning of Species Occurrences in Environmental Space
text: Instead arbitrary thinning, find_env_resolution() selects kernel-density bandwidth environmental variable (Sheather–Jones plug-default, Silverman Scott rules also available). bandwidth statistically defensible choice edge length environmental grid cell: scale empirical density observations becomes smooth. See Finding environmental resolution vignette.

title: Data Thinning of Species Occurrences in Environmental Space
text: bean offers two core thinning methods: Stochastic (thin_env_nd): Randomly samples one “bean” occupied “pod”. Deterministic (thin_env_center): Generates new point exact center every occupied grid cell. See Apply thinning vignette.

title: Data Thinning of Species Occurrences in Environmental Space
text: fit_ellipsoid() function formalizes environmental niche fitting bivariate multivariate ellipse around thinned points. See Niche delineation vignette.

title: Data Thinning of Species Occurrences in Environmental Space
text: Using learned niche, predict() projects results back geographic space. step emphasizes ellipsoid-based approach used calculate suitability scores delineated niche boundaries. See Prediction mapping vignette.

title: Data Thinning of Species Occurrences in Environmental Space
text: full demonstrations protocol, check package vignettes: End ❤️
code: # Data Preparation & Visualization vignette("data-preparation") #> Warning: vignette 'data-preparation' not found  # Objective Thinning in Environmental Space vignette("environmental-thinning") #> Warning: vignette 'environmental-thinning' not found  # Niche Delineation & Suitability Mapping vignette("niche-modeling") #> Warning: vignette 'niche-modeling' not found

what: bean: Data Thinning of Species Occurrences in Environmental Space — bean-package
title: bean: Data Thinning of Species Occurrences in Environmental Space — bean-package
text: suite tools mitigate sampling bias species occurrence records thinning data environmental space (E-space). process can improve accuracy precision species distribution models (SDM, also known ecological niche models, ENM). package offers data-driven protocol determine thinning parameters using kernel-density bandwidth selection. Two thinning methods provided (stochastic deterministic) reduce -sampled environmental conditions -weight outlier observations. name 'bean' reflects core principle method: 'pod' (grid cell E-space) allowed contain limited number 'beans' (occurrence points). See Silverman (1986, ISBN:978-0-412-24620-3) Rousseeuw Leroy (2003, ISBN:978-0-471-48855-2) underlying statistical methods.

title: bean: Data Thinning of Species Occurrences in Environmental Space — bean-package
text: Maintainer: Paanwaris Paansri paanwaris@vt.edu (ORCID) Authors: Paanwaris Paansri paanwaris@vt.edu (ORCID) Luis E. Escobar escobar1@vt.edu (ORCID) [contributor]

what: Find an objective environmental grid resolution — find_env_resolution
title: Find an objective environmental grid resolution — find_env_resolution
text: Calculates objective, data-driven grid resolution environmental thinning. environmental variable, function selects bandwidth kernel-density estimate (KDE) marginal distribution. chosen bandwidth defines spatial scale two observations carry essentially redundant information, therefore natural choice edge length environmental grid cell. Three established bandwidth selectors supported (see Details): "sheather-jones" (default) — Sheather–Jones direct         plug-estimator (Sheather & Jones, 1991), modern recommended         default non-Gaussian data; "silverman" — Silverman's rule thumb (Silverman, 1986); "scott" — Scott's rule (Scott, 1992).

title: Find an objective environmental grid resolution — find_env_resolution
code: find_env_resolution(   data,   env_vars,   method = c("sheather-jones", "silverman", "scott") )

title: Find an objective environmental grid resolution — find_env_resolution
text: data data.frame containing environmental variables. env_vars character vector specifying environmental variables analyse. method bandwidth selector. One "sheather-jones" (default), "silverman", "scott".

title: Find an objective environmental grid resolution — find_env_resolution
text: object class bean_env_resolution (list) : suggested_resolution named numeric vector           suggested grid resolution variable, units           variable. bandwidths bandwidths used derive resolution           (identical suggested_resolution). density_data long-format data.frame kernel           density estimates, used plot.bean_env_resolution. method bandwidth selector used.

title: Find an objective environmental grid resolution — find_env_resolution
text: bandwidth? good environmental grid cell small enough distinguish ecologically meaningful differences, large enough absorb sampling noise. kernel density bandwidth chosen data answers exactly question: scale empirical density observations becomes smooth. Using grid resolution yields one occurrence per cell average sampling intensity near mode data. Selectors. Sheather–Jones (stats::bw.SJ method = "dpi")         plug-selector robust non-Gaussian densities         standard recommendation modern literature (Sheather &         Jones, 1991; Jones, Marron & Sheather, 1996). Recommended default. Silverman (stats::bw.nrd0) rule--thumb         \(h = 0.9 \, \min(\hat\sigma, IQR/1.34) \, n^{-1/5}\) (Silverman,         1986). Fast stable, assumes near-Gaussian shape. Scott (stats::bw.nrd) Gaussian-optimal rule         \(h = 1.06 \, \hat\sigma \, n^{-1/5}\) (Scott, 1992). Simpler         Silverman less robust outliers. "sheather-jones" fails (can happen strongly tied data), function falls back Silverman's rule variable emits message().

title: Find an objective environmental grid resolution — find_env_resolution
text: Sheather, S. J. & Jones, M. C. (1991). reliable data-based bandwidth selection method kernel density estimation. Journal Royal Statistical Society: Series B, 53(3), 683–690. Silverman, B. W. (1986). Density Estimation Statistics Data Analysis. Chapman & Hall, London. Scott, D. W. (1992). Multivariate Density Estimation: Theory, Practice, Visualization. Wiley, New York. Jones, M. C., Marron, J. S. & Sheather, S. J. (1996). brief survey bandwidth selection density estimation. Journal American Statistical Association, 91(433), 401–407.

title: Find an objective environmental grid resolution — find_env_resolution
code: set.seed(1) df <- data.frame(   bio1  = c(rnorm(200, 15, 2), rnorm(50, 25, 1)),   bio12 = c(rnorm(200, 1200, 200), rnorm(50, 2500, 100)) ) res <- find_env_resolution(df, env_vars = c("bio1", "bio12")) res$suggested_resolution #>       bio1      bio12  #>  0.7134594 74.0651811  plot(res)

what: Fit an environmental niche ellipsoid — fit_ellipsoid
title: Fit an environmental niche ellipsoid — fit_ellipsoid
text: Fits ellipsoid encompasses chosen proportion data points environmental space two dimensions. centroid covariance matrix can estimated either classical sample moments ("covmat") robust Minimum Volume Ellipsoid ("mve"; Rousseeuw, 1985). Points classified inside outside ellipsoid using \(\chi^2\) cutoff squared Mahalanobis distance.

title: Fit an environmental niche ellipsoid — fit_ellipsoid
code: fit_ellipsoid(data, env_vars, method = "covmat", level = 0.95)

title: Fit an environmental niche ellipsoid — fit_ellipsoid
text: data data.frame containing environmental variables. env_vars character vector least two column names data representing environmental variables. method One "covmat" (default, classical) "mve" (robust Minimum Volume Ellipsoid via cov.mve). level single number (0, 1): confidence level ellipsoid. Default 0.95.

title: Fit an environmental niche ellipsoid — fit_ellipsoid
text: object class bean_ellipsoid (list) : centroid Named vector variable means / centre. covariance_matrix covariance matrix used. niche_ellipse data.frame polygon vertices           2-D ellipse. NULL two variables           supplied (3-D mesh generated lazily plot). all_points_used Complete-case input data. points_in_ellipse Subset inside ellipsoid. points_outside_ellipse Subset outside ellipsoid. inside_indices Row indices (all_points_used)           classified inside. parameters List level method.

title: Fit an environmental niche ellipsoid — fit_ellipsoid
text: Methods. "covmat" uses sample mean sample covariance matrix. optimal multivariate normality sensitive outliers. "mve" (Rousseeuw, 1985) finds smallest-volume ellipsoid contains fraction data robust moderate proportion contaminating points. Confidence level. Assuming approximate multivariate normality, boundary ellipsoid set points whose squared Mahalanobis distance equals qchisq(level, df = n_dim).

title: Fit an environmental niche ellipsoid — fit_ellipsoid
text: Rousseeuw, P. J. (1985). Multivariate estimation high breakdown point. Mathematical Statistics Applications, Vol. B, 283–297. Van Aelst, S. & Rousseeuw, P. (2009). Minimum volume ellipsoid. Wiley Interdisciplinary Reviews: Computational Statistics, 1(1), 71–82. Cobos, M. E., Osorio-Olvera, L., Soberón, J., Peterson, . T., Barve, V. & Barve, N. (2024). ellipsenm: ecological niches' characterizations using ellipsoids. https://github.com/marlonecobos/ellipsenm.

title: Fit an environmental niche ellipsoid — fit_ellipsoid
code: set.seed(81) env_data <- data.frame(   BIO1  = c(rnorm(50, 10, 1), 30),   BIO12 = c(rnorm(50, 20, 2), 50) ) fit <- fit_ellipsoid(env_data, env_vars = c("BIO1", "BIO12"),                      method = "covmat", level = 0.95) print(fit) #> -- Bean Environmental Niche Ellipsoid -- #> Method      : covmat #> Dimensions  : 2 (BIO1, BIO12) #> Level       : 95.00% #> Points used : 51  (inside: 49, 96.1%) #> Centroid: #>     BIO1    BIO12  #> 10.48281 20.99832  plot(fit)

what: Visualize n-dimensional environmental thinning results — plot_bean
title: Visualize n-dimensional environmental thinning results — plot_bean
text: function creates scatterplot matrix (pairs plot)   visualize results n-dimensional environmental thinning using base R   graphics. can accept thinned objects either density-based thinning   (`thin_env_nd`) deterministic centroid thinning (`thin_env_center`).

title: Visualize n-dimensional environmental thinning results — plot_bean
code: plot_bean(original_data, thinned_object, env_vars)

title: Visualize n-dimensional environmental thinning results — plot_bean
text: original_data data.frame prepared, unthinned occurrence points. thinned_object output object `thin_env_nd()` `thin_env_center()`. env_vars character vector environmental variables plot.

title: Visualize n-dimensional environmental thinning results — plot_bean
text: Invisibly returns `NULL`. Draws plot active graphics device.

title: Visualize n-dimensional environmental thinning results — plot_bean
code: data(origin_dat_prepared, package = "bean") env_vars <- c("bio_1", "bio_12") thinned <- thin_env_nd(   data            = origin_dat_prepared,   env_vars        = env_vars,   grid_resolution = c(0.5, 0.5),   seed            = 1 ) plot_bean(origin_dat_prepared, thinned, env_vars = env_vars)

what: Predict suitability and Mahalanobis distance from a bean ellipsoid — predict.bean_ellipsoid
title: Predict suitability and Mahalanobis distance from a bean ellipsoid — predict.bean_ellipsoid
text: Computes Mahalanobis distance suitability values deriving fitted bean_ellipsoid object new environmental data.

title: Predict suitability and Mahalanobis distance from a bean ellipsoid — predict.bean_ellipsoid
code: # S3 method for class 'bean_ellipsoid' predict(   object,   newdata,   include_suitability = TRUE,   suitability_truncated = FALSE,   include_mahalanobis = TRUE,   mahalanobis_truncated = FALSE,   keep_data = NULL,   ... )

title: Predict suitability and Mahalanobis distance from a bean ellipsoid — predict.bean_ellipsoid
text: object object class bean_ellipsoid. newdata Environmental predictors. Can data.frame, matrix, terra::SpatRaster. Must contain column/layer names matching variables used fit ellipsoid. include_suitability (logical) TRUE (default), returns continuous suitability values. suitability_truncated (logical) TRUE, returns truncated suitability layer values outside chi-square contour set 0. Default = FALSE. include_mahalanobis (logical) TRUE (default), returns continuous Mahalanobis distance. mahalanobis_truncated (logical) TRUE, returns truncated Mahalanobis layer values outside chi-square contour set NA. Default = FALSE. keep_data (logical) TRUE, includes original predictors output. Default FALSE SpatRaster TRUE data.frames. ... Additional arguments (unused).

title: Predict suitability and Mahalanobis distance from a bean ellipsoid — predict.bean_ellipsoid
text: data.frame SpatRaster (matching input type newdata)   containing requested prediction layers.

what: Prepare data for environmental thinning — prepare_bean
title: Prepare data for environmental thinning — prepare_bean
text: function serves pre-processing step clean prepare species occurrence data. performs three key actions: 1. Removes records missing longitude latitude values. 2. Extracts environmental data raster layers already scaled    occurrence point. 3. Removes records fall outside raster extent missing    environmental data. final output clean data frame environmental variables mean 0 standard deviation 1.

title: Prepare data for environmental thinning — prepare_bean
code: prepare_bean(   data,   env_rasters,   longitude,   latitude,   transform = c("scale", "pca", "none") )

title: Prepare data for environmental thinning — prepare_bean
text: data data.frame species occurrences records, including columns longitude latitude. env_rasters SpatRaster (terra package) RasterStack (raster package) object environmental variables. longitude (character) name longitude column data. latitude (character) name latitude column data. transform (character) transformation apply environmental rasters extracting data. Options "scale" (default), "pca", "none". See Details.

title: Prepare data for environmental thinning — prepare_bean
text: data.frame containing cleaned scaled occurrence data,   following columns: Original Columns columns input data preserved valid records. Environmental Variables New columns, named layers env_rasters, containing extracted scaled environmental data.

title: Prepare data for environmental thinning — prepare_bean
text: ### Environmental Variable Transformation transform argument allows different pre-processing environmental raster layers address issues like differing units multicollinearity. - "scale" (Default):** standard approach handle variables   different units (e.g., °C vs. mm). transforms raster layer   mean 0 standard deviation 1 (Baddeley et al., 2016). process makes   variables equal variance. result,   variable contributes equally analysis, ensuring   resulting resolutions based relative distribution data points   within environmental dimension, arbitrary original units   (Beaugrand, 2024; Kléparski et al., 2021). - "pca": option performs Principal Component Analysis (PCA)   environmental rasters. powerful technique dealing   multicollinearity (highly correlated variables). transforms original   rasters new set uncorrelated layers (Principal Components) (Qiao et al., 2016).   function extracts PC scores occurrence point. - "none": option extracts raw environmental values   rasters without transformation. suitable rasters   already scaled specific reason use raw values.

title: Prepare data for environmental thinning — prepare_bean
text: Baddeley, ., Rubak, E. Turner, R. (2016). Spatial point patterns: methodology applications R. CRC press. Beaugrand, G. (2024). ecological niche model considers local relationships among variables: Environmental String Model. Ecosphere, 15(10), e70015. Kléparski, L., Beaugrand, G. Edwards, M. (2021). Plankton biogeography North Atlantic Ocean adjacent seas: Species assemblages environmental signatures. Ecology Evolution, 11(10), 5135-5149. Qiao, H., Peterson, . T., Campbell, L. P., Soberón, J., Ji, L. Escobar, L. E. (2016). NicheA: creating virtual species ecological niches multivariate environmental scenarios. Ecography, 39(8), 805-813.

title: Prepare data for environmental thinning — prepare_bean
code: # \donttest{ env_file <- system.file("extdata", "thai_env.tif", package = "bean") occ_file <- system.file("extdata", "Rusa_unicolor.csv", package = "bean") if (nzchar(env_file) && nzchar(occ_file) &&     requireNamespace("terra", quietly = TRUE)) {   env <- terra::rast(env_file)   occ <- read.csv(occ_file)   prepared <- prepare_bean(     data        = occ,     env_rasters = env,     longitude   = "x",     latitude    = "y",     transform   = "scale"   )   head(prepared) } #> Scaling environmental rasters... #> Extracting environmental data for occurrence points... #> 5 records removed because they fell outside the raster extent or had NA environmental values. #> Data preparation complete. Returning 1024 clean records. #>         species        y         x      bio_1       bio_12     bio_15 #> 1 Rusa unicolor 15.37239  99.11555 -1.6909295  0.003511156 -0.2454693 #> 2 Rusa unicolor 15.41415  99.28763 -0.8711075 -0.267821213 -0.3053829 #> 3 Rusa unicolor 14.46838 101.22005 -1.3879976 -0.534812263 -0.3835742 #> 4 Rusa unicolor 15.65606  99.31600 -0.6288324 -0.224408034 -0.2390396 #> 5 Rusa unicolor 14.39543 101.41694 -2.0311866 -0.604273349 -0.5074636 #> 6 Rusa unicolor 12.72500 100.88947  1.0537073 -0.311234392 -0.6623129 #>        bio_4 #> 1 -0.2573387 #> 2 -0.1768698 #> 3 -0.2876102 #> 4 -0.0834852 #> 5 -0.1398570 #> 6 -1.0746857 # }

title: Print a summary of bean_thinned_center results — print.bean_thinned_center
text: Invisibly returns input object x.

title: Deterministic centroid — thin_env_center
text: function thins species occurrence records finding occupied cells 2D environmental grid returning single new point exact center cells. deterministic method.

title: Deterministic centroid — thin_env_center
code: thin_env_center(data, env_vars, grid_resolution)

title: Deterministic centroid — thin_env_center
text: data data.frame containing species occurrence coordinates environmental variables. env_vars character vector specifying column names data represent environmental variables used analysis. grid_resolution numeric vector length one two specifying resolution(s) grid axes. length one, used axes.

title: Deterministic centroid — thin_env_center
text: object class bean_thinned_center. list containing: thinned_points data.frame two columns representing new     points center occupied environmental grid cell. n_original integer representing number complete occurrence     records input data. n_thinned integer representing number unique grid cells     occupied, also number points returned. parameters list key parameters used, whether     scaling applied.

title: Deterministic centroid — thin_env_center
code: env_data <- data.frame(   BIO1  = c(0.1, 0.2, 1.1, 1.2, 1.3),   BIO12 = c(0.1, 0.2, 2.1, 2.2, 2.3) ) thin_env_center(env_data, env_vars = c("BIO1", "BIO12"),                 grid_resolution = c(0.1, 0.2)) #> --- Bean Deterministic Thinning Results --- #>  #> Thinned 5 original points to 5 unique grid cell centers. #> This represents a retention of 100.0% of the data. #>  #> --------------------------------------

what: Thin occurrence data in n-dimensional environmental space — thin_env_nd
title: Thin occurrence data in n-dimensional environmental space — thin_env_nd
text: function thins species occurrence records n-dimensional environmental space randomly sampling exactly one point occupied n-dimensional grid cell (hypercube).

title: Thin occurrence data in n-dimensional environmental space — thin_env_nd
code: thin_env_nd(data, env_vars, grid_resolution, seed = NULL)

title: Thin occurrence data in n-dimensional environmental space — thin_env_nd
text: data data.frame containing species occurrences pre-scaled environmental variables, typically output `prepare_bean()`. env_vars character vector two column names representing environmental variables (dimensions) use thinning. grid_resolution numeric vector resolutions environmental axis. length must match length `env_vars`. seed (numeric) optional random seed reproducibility. provided, random number generator state safely isolated function call affect global environment. Default = NULL.

title: Thin occurrence data in n-dimensional environmental space — thin_env_nd
text: object class `bean_thinned`, list containing: thinned_data data.frame containing occurrence records retained thinning process. n_original integer representing number complete occurrence records input data thinning. n_thinned integer representing number occurrence records remaining thinning. parameters list key parameters used thinning process.

title: Thin occurrence data in n-dimensional environmental space — thin_env_nd
code: data(origin_dat_prepared, package = "bean") thinned <- thin_env_nd(   data            = origin_dat_prepared,   env_vars        = c("bio_1", "bio_12"),   grid_resolution = c(0.5, 0.5),   seed            = 123 ) print(thinned) #> --- Bean Stochastic Thinning Results --- #>  #> Thinned 1024 original points to 77 points. #> This represents a retention of 7.5% of the data. #>  #> --------------------------------------

title: Deterministically thinned environmental data — thinned_deterministic
text: Result thin_env_center applied origin_dat_prepared. Contains one calculated centroid per occupied environmental grid cell.

title: Deterministically thinned environmental data — thinned_deterministic
code: thinned_deterministic

title: Deterministically thinned environmental data — thinned_deterministic
text: bean_thinned_center object (see   thin_env_center).

title: Stochastically thinned environmental data — thinned_stochastic
text: Result thin_env_nd applied origin_dat_prepared. Contains one randomly chosen occurrence per occupied environmental grid cell.

title: Stochastically thinned environmental data — thinned_stochastic
code: thinned_stochastic

title: Stochastically thinned environmental data — thinned_stochastic
text: bean_thinned object (see thin_env_nd).

title: bean 0.2.0
text: find_env_resolution() longer uses geometric “elbow” rule nearest- neighbour distances. now selects kernel-density bandwidth environmental variable using Sheather-Jones plug-estimator (default), Silverman Scott rules also available via new method argument. bandwidth statistically defensible choice edge length environmental grid cell, new implementation faster robust ties. rgl now Suggests dependency rather Imports. 3-D plots still work rgl installed; otherwise plot.bean_ellipsoid() falls back 2-D view first two requested dimensions. S3 method predict.bean_ellipsoid() now documented canonical name (previously help page generated predict.ellipsoid_bean). bean-overview — quickstart introduction. data-preparation — preparing rasters occurrences. environmental-thinning — resolution selection thinning. niche-modeling — fitting ellipsoids projecting suitability. vignettes now build without requiring rgl.

title: bean 0.2.0
text: DESCRIPTION: trimmed Imports MASS, stats, terra; moved rgl, ggplot2 dplyr Suggests (dplyr removed entirely package — used one vignette). Added tests/testthat/ test suite covering find_env_resolution(), thin_env_nd(), thin_env_center(), fit_ellipsoid() predict.bean_ellipsoid(). Datasets now documented user-visible (\keyword{internal}). Replaced \dontrun{} examples depended missing files \donttest{} examples use shipped sample data. prepare_bean() now uses match.arg() transform. Cleaned R/globals.R (carrying many unused identifiers).

title: bean 0.2.0
text: print.bean_ellipsoid() output reformatted clarity. Internal helper .bean_ellipse_polygon() consolidates 2-D ellipse polygon code path used fit_ellipsoid() plot.bean_ellipsoid().

title: bean 0.1.2
text: Earlier development versions; see commit history GitHub.
